verdict_reasoning: SRC is a canonical cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase with no inherent membrane anchor. However, it undergoes N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation, anchoring it to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane and intracellular membranes. Critically, tumor cells display documented cancer-cell-restricted ecto-pools of SRC and other Src-family kinases on the outer leaflet, where they phosphorylate extracellular substrates and serve as therapeutic targets (e.g., dasatinib-based ADCs, surface-directed kinase inhibitors). This cancer-state surface translocation, alongside documented cell-death-induced and activation-induced externalization in certain immune contexts, qualifies SRC as contextual surface-accessible under non-baseline oncogenic or stress conditions.
